LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MAGNOLIADRHOMES LLC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF MAGNOLIADRHOMES LLC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT UNDULY DELAYED IN SEEKING LEAVE TO AMEND, AND DEFENDANTS DO NOT SHOW THAT THEY WOULD BE PREJUDICED BY THE AMENDMENT. ANY REQUEST TO COORDINATE, STAY OR TRANSFER THE ACTION MUST BE BROUGHT BY SEPARATE MOTION. LIKEWISE, ANY REQUEST TO CONTINUE THE EXISTING TRIAL DATE MUST BE MADE IN DEPT. 206. ORDER SIGNED. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR NO. 5041, EMAIL: RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM. (302/EPS)

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One) From Blueprint Investment Fund, Llc
Off Calendar Sep-30-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM
DHAIVAT H. SHAH, ESQ; (408) 255-6310; GRELLAS SHAH LLP; ds@grellas.com; for Plaintiff MAGNOLIADRHOMES LLC,.
ANDREW D. CASTRICON. Esq; (415) 986-5900; GORDON REES SCULLY MANSUKHANI, LLP; for Defendants, PEAK FINANCIAL COMPANY AND JOSEPH W. LIBKEY, JR..
MICHAEL BROOKS CARROLL, Esq; (415) 788-7600; LAW OFFICES OF MICHAEL BROOKS CARROLL; for Defendants, BLUEPRINT INVESTMENT FUND LLC,
Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Court reporter,

Plaintiff and Defendants contests the Court's tentative ruling.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, Court modifies the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

PLAINTIFFS MAGNOLIADRHOMES LLC, MAGNOLIADRHOMES LLC, YOULIN WANG'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One) From Blueprint Investment Fund, LLC.

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted in its entirety. Within 21 days, defendants shall serve further responses which comply with the Court's May 19, 2021 order, and Defendants shall pay monetary sanctions in the amount of $9,055. Defendants are admonished that further willful discovery violations may result in issue, evidentiary, or terminating sanctions. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 2030.290(c); Liberty Mutual Fire Ins. Co. v. LcL Administrators, Inc. (2008) 163 Cal.App.4th 1093.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/EPS)

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Dhaivat H. Shah, Esq. of Grellas Shah LLP, 408.255.6310. for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. Nima Nayebi, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani LLP, 415.986.5900, for the Defendants.

PLAINTIFFS MAGNOLIADRHOMES LLC and YOULIN WANG's Motion to Compel further responses to form interrogatories (set one) and to requests for production and to compel compliance with requests for production.

The matter is called. ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling with a modification ordering that the Defendants comply within 30 days:

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories set one and to requests for production and compliance with requests for production is granted as set forth in the report and recommendation.

The Defendants have 30 days to comply.

Order signed as modified.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

Notice And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-16-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF MAGNOLIADRHOMES LLC's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.

No appearances in this matter. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Plaintiff Magnoliadrhomes LLC's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted. Plaintiff has not unduly delayed in seeking leave to amend, and Defendants do not show that they would be prejudiced by the amendment. To the extent that Defendants challenge the viability of the proposed causes of action or the court's jurisdiction to decide those causes of action, they may raise those issues by demurrer or other appropriate motion. (See, e.g., California Casualty Gen. Ins. v. Superior Court (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 ["the preferable practice would be to permit the amendment and allow the parties to test its legal sufficiency by demurrer, motion for judgment on the pleadings or other appropriate proceedings."].) The Court will not condition leave to amend on plaintiff's agreement to "consolidate" this action with a related action pending in a different court. Any request to coordinate, stay or transfer the action must be brought by separate motion. Likewise, any request to continue the existing trial date must be made in Dept. 206.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/EPS)
